# forceReplace

**Instance Method**

Replaces one or more records with the specified records regardless of conflicts.

## Declaration

```swift
CloudKit.RecordsBatchBuilder forceReplace(
	CloudKit.Record|CloudKit.Record[] records,
	optional Object options
);
```

## Parameters

- **records**: A [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.Record] dictionary representing the replacement record, if you are replacing a single record. If you are replacing multiple records, this value is an array of replacement [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.Record] dictionaries. The `recordChangeTag` key is not required in the [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.Record] dictionaries. Only the values of the fields in these dictionaries are replaced. The other field values are set to `null`.
- **options**: A dictionary containing options for this operation. This parameter contains a single `desiredKeys` key that is an array of field names (`String` values). Only the fields specified in the array are set.

## Return Value

The object that received this method call.

## Discussion

Creates records if they don’t exist.

## Replacing Records

- **replace**: Replaces one or more records with the specified records.
